takeaways: Welltower posted record 22.1% Q1 SHOP same-store NOI growth and raised 2026 guidance to $6.21–$6.35 normalized FFO — operator-level economics in senior housing are at their best in years.
The Fed cut to 3.50–3.75%, but the 10-Year Treasury is anchored near 4.00–4.25%, leaving CRE borrowing costs only modestly easier — yet 2026 transaction volume is still forecast up 16% to $562B.

Agentic AI funding has hit $2.66B across 44 rounds in 2026 (+142% YoY) as proptech shifts from pilots to deployments — RET Ventures, Jitty and others are betting on automation of leasing and resident workflows.

items.headline: Welltower posts record 22.1% SHOP SSNOI growth, raises 2026 guidance
items.summary: Welltower's Q1 2026 normalized FFO climbed 23% to $1.47/share on 16.4% total same-store NOI growth and 22.1% SHOP SSNOI growth — its 14th straight quarter above 20%. Management raised full-year FFO guidance to $6.21–$6.35 and closed $3.2B across 41 deals adding 4,200+ units, signaling confidence senior housing operators can keep compounding NOI.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy on track to break 91.3% all-time record in 2026
items.summary: Industry experts project 2026 occupancy will surpass 90% and may eclipse the 91.3% high set in 2007, driven by 17 straight quarters of net absorption outpacing new openings. With NIC MAP showing inventory growth of just 0.7% and a 29-month construction cycle, the supply-demand imbalance favors existing operators like Lloyd Jones through at least 2028.

items.headline: Fed cuts to 3.50–3.75% but CRE borrowing costs stay tethered to 10-Year
items.summary: The Fed delivered a 25-bp cut to 3.50–3.75% with Powell flagging a softer-than-headline labor market. But fixed-rate CRE loans price off the 10-Year Treasury, which is stuck at 4.00–4.25% — so refi pressure on 2021-vintage debt persists, especially in office and increasingly multifamily where loan defaults are up significantly.

items.headline: CRE investment forecast to climb 16% to $562B in 2026, near pre-pandemic norm
items.summary: Colliers and CBRE see 2026 sales volume rising 15–20% as institutional and cross-border capital re-enters, with total CRE investment projected at $562B. The MBA expects a 24% jump in CRE lending volume; average LTVs near 61% and debt yields of 9.8% suggest underwriting discipline is holding even as liquidity returns.

items.headline: Agentic AI funding hits $2.66B in 2026 YTD, up 142% year over year
items.summary: Agentic AI startups raised $2.66B across 44 rounds through April 2026 — a 142.6% jump versus 2025. In real estate specifically, capital is targeting agent productivity, leasing automation and resident communications, with proptech moving from experimentation to production deployment as buyers demand measurable ROI rather than 'AI-washed' features.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report rising stress, 40% considering exit as orgs run lean
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership survey finds 71% of leaders under increased stress and 40% considering leaving — at the same moment U.S. layoffs hit a 10-year high and middle-management layers are being flattened. The implication for operators: bench depth, succession planning, and burnout mitigation are now strategic risks, not HR concerns.

items.headline: Ventas adds 29 senior housing communities for $983M in Q1 2026
items.summary: Ventas continued aggressive SHOP expansion, acquiring 29 senior housing communities for ~$983.4M in Q1 while divesting 12 properties for $47.1M. The pace mirrors Welltower's $3.2B Q1 spend, confirming the public REITs are racing to lock down operating portfolios before the 2026–2028 demand wave arrives.

items.headline: Senior living operators dial back rental rate increases for 2026
items.summary: After several years of double-digit rate growth, operators are taking a more moderate approach to 2026 rental increases as affordability concerns mount and consumer price sensitivity rises. The shift suggests the next leg of NOI growth will come from occupancy gains and expense discipline rather than headline rate hikes.

items.headline: Multifamily Q1 volume flat at $32B but individual asset sales tick up 3%
items.summary: U.S. multifamily transaction volume held essentially flat year over year at $32B in Q1 2026, with single-asset deals rising 3% — a sign price discovery is closing between buyers and sellers. Boston led the regional rebound, with Q1 volume up more than 50% YoY, while several public REIT take-privates re-emerged.

items.headline: Fannie and Freddie multifamily caps lifted 20% to $88B each for 2026
items.summary: FHFA raised the 2026 multifamily lending caps to $88B for each agency — a 20% increase that injects $35B+ of incremental agency liquidity into the multifamily market. Combined with the MBA's 24% lending growth forecast, this is a meaningful tailwind for refinancing 2021–2022 vintage loans coming due.

items.headline: RET Ventures launches AI Accelerator for rental-tech startups
items.summary: RET Ventures kicked off its inaugural AI Accelerator Program in April with first-cohort participants LeasingAI and brightplace, focused on leasing automation and apartment marketing. The program signals where institutional rental owners expect the next productivity gains — front-of-funnel leasing and resident communications.

items.headline: Jitty raises $3.8M seed for LLM-driven property search platform
items.summary: Real estate startup Jitty raised $3.8M in seed funding for an AI search platform that uses LLMs to help renters and buyers interpret housing value and neighborhood trends. The round is part of a broader resurgence in residential proptech that Fifth Wall and others are backing as agent productivity tools take center stage.

items.headline: U.S. layoffs surge to 10-year high as orgs enter 2026 lean
items.summary: Layoffs across U.S. industries are at the highest point in a decade, with many organizations entering 2026 in a 'very lean state' after AI-driven efficiency cuts. The thinning of middle-management layers is creating communication breakdowns and burnout, raising the bar for succession planning at every operating company.

items.headline: Leadership development named top HR priority entering 2026
items.summary: HR leaders rank leadership development as their #1 priority for 2026, with enabling innovation rapidly rising as a co-priority. Successful leaders will be those balancing AI integration with human judgment, navigating complexity without clear roadmaps, and making learning a retention lever rather than a perk.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of employees say wellness drives higher performance
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness report finds 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, and 91% say wellness spaces help them handle work pressure. For operators of resident-facing communities, this reframes amenity investment as productivity infrastructure — and a hiring magnet for staff.

items.headline: 'Emotional fitness' emerges as the next wellness category for 2026
items.summary: The Global Wellness Institute identifies emotional fitness — combining cognitive performance and emotional resilience — as a new mainstream wellness vertical for 2026, alongside neurowellness focused on brain aging. Senior living programming and lifestyle marketing should expect demand for resilience-oriented offerings, not just fitness.

items.headline: Senior housing inventory growth stalls at 0.7%, construction cycles stretch to 29 months
items.summary: NIC MAP data shows annual senior housing inventory growth of just 0.7% in late 2025, with the average construction cycle now 29 months — meaning anything breaking ground in 2026 won't open before 2028. The constrained pipeline locks in pricing power for existing operators.

items.headline: NAR survey: 46% of agents say AI has had little to no business impact
items.summary: A National Association of Realtors technology survey finds 46% of agents report AI has had no meaningful effect on their business — highlighting the gap between AI hype and practical adoption. The takeaway for proptech: solutions still need to prove ROI before broad uptake, and 'AI-washed' tools won't cut it.

items.headline: Personalized wellness goes mainstream with AI biomarkers and at-home tests
items.summary: 2026 marks the inflection point for personalized wellness as AI-driven analytics, at-home biomarker tests, and tracking devices move beyond steps and sleep. Self-care routines tailored to individual physiology — from skincare to nutrition — are now the consumer baseline, raising the table stakes for amenity programs.

items.headline: Longevity training shifts from biohacker niche to mainstream lifestyle
items.summary: Longevity and metabolic health are moving from biohacker fringe to mainstream as people prioritize functional capacity for decades, not years. Classes blending mobility, single-leg strength, cross-body patterns and reaction training are spreading — useful programming intelligence for senior living lifestyle teams.
